// <auto-generated />
// This file is auto-generated by Mutty.

#nullable enable

using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Collections.Immutable;
using System.Linq;

namespace Mutty.Tests
{
    /// <summary>
    /// The mutable wrapper for the <see cref="Stats"/> record.
    /// </summary>
    public partial class MutableStats
    {
        private Stats _record;

        /// <summary>
        /// Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="MutableStats"/> class.
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="record">The record to wrap.</param>
        public MutableStats(Stats record)
        {
            _record = record;

            Score = _record.Score;
            OptionalScore = _record.OptionalScore;
            Rating = _record.Rating;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Builds a new instance of the <see cref="Stats"/> class.
        /// </summary>
        public Stats Build()
        {
            OnBeforeBuild();
            return _record with
            {
                Score = this.Score,
                OptionalScore = this.OptionalScore,
                Rating = this.Rating,
            };
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Builds a new instance of the <see cref="Stats"/> record. Alias for <see cref="Build"/>.
        /// </summary>
        public Stats ToImmutable()
        {
            return Build();
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Called at the start of <see cref="Build"/>, before the immutable record is produced.
        /// </summary>
        /// Implement this method in a user-defined partial class to validate or normalise state.
        partial void OnBeforeBuild();

        /// <summary>
        /// Performs an implicit conversion from <see cref="Stats"/> to <see cref="MutableStats"/>.
        /// </summary>
        public static implicit operator MutableStats(Stats record)
        {
            return new MutableStats(record);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Performs an explicit conversion from <see cref="MutableStats"/> to <see cref="Stats"/>.
        /// </summary>
        /// <remarks>Explicit because it allocates a new record; prefer <see cref="Build"/>.</remarks>
        public static explicit operator Stats(MutableStats mutable)
        {
            return mutable.Build();
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Gets or sets the Score.
        /// </summary>
        public int Score { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Gets or sets the OptionalScore.
        /// </summary>
        public int? OptionalScore { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Gets or sets the Rating.
        /// </summary>
        public double? Rating { get; set; }

        /// <summary>
        /// Sets <see cref="Score"/> and returns this wrapper so calls can be chained.
        /// </summary>
        public MutableStats WithScore(int value)
        {
            Score = value;
            return this;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Sets <see cref="OptionalScore"/> and returns this wrapper so calls can be chained.
        /// </summary>
        public MutableStats WithOptionalScore(int? value)
        {
            OptionalScore = value;
            return this;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Sets <see cref="Rating"/> and returns this wrapper so calls can be chained.
        /// </summary>
        public MutableStats WithRating(double? value)
        {
            Rating = value;
            return this;
        }
    }
}
